summary: iter-99 RETEST (backend only). Devanagari matra fix + extend_days cap verified. Backend pytest 40/40 PASS on the iter-99 suite (extended from 27 → 40 by adding 7 cross-script names + 4 cap boundary tests + retaining original 29). _NAME_RE in /app/backend/routes/auth.py was expanded with explicit Indic block ranges (\u0900-\u097F Devanagari + Bengali \u0980-\u09FF + Gurmukhi \u0A00-\u0A7F + Gujarati \u0A80-\u0AFF + Odia \u0B00-\u0B7F + Tamil \u0B80-\u0BFF + Telugu \u0C00-\u0C7F + Kannada \u0C80-\u0CFF + Malayalam \u0D00-\u0D7F + Latin combining diacritics \u0300-\u036F) — every cross-script smoke name now returns 200 from POST /api/auth/profile: 'राहुल', 'अमित', 'नमस्ते', 'অমিত', 'அமித்', 'અમિત', 'ਅਮਿਤ', 'అమిత్', 'ಅಮಿತ್', 'അമിത്'. Server-side extend_days cap |x| ≤ 3650 verified at /api/admin/users/{id}/wallet-adjust: +5000 → 400 with detail 'extend_days must be between −3650 and +3650'; -5000 → 400 same detail; +3650 → 200 (boundary OK); -3650 → 200 (boundary OK). All other iter-99 MUST FRs still green: Rushikesh original payload 200, name accepts digits/hyphen/apostrophe/dots/2-80 chars, name rejects empty/1-char/>80 chars, address <10 → 400, address ≥10 → 200, phone validation unchanged (rejects leading 1-5/non-10-digit/non-numeric; accepts leading 6-9), signed extend_days -5 pulls back 5d, -3000 floors at start_date (note: floor test value lowered from -9999 to -3000 since new cap rejects ≤-3650 — sub spans only 30d so -3000 still floors), audit persists signed -7, positive +5 still works. Regression suites also green: iter-98 meals_delta ± (2/2), iter-97 silent branch (5/5), iter-92 franchise scoping (17/17). TEST_IT99 seed docs all purged in teardown.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/auth.py:148-164 — Indic block expansion is correct and minimal; \u0900-\u097F covers Devanagari letters AND matras AND virama in one range so no need for separate \p{M} support. Comment is now accurate.
routes/auth.py:165 — _PHONE_RE strips '+91'/space/hyphen before match — good; but does not strip leading '0' (some users type 0XXXXXXXXX). Low-priority enhancement, not a regression.
server.py:2293-2294 — Cap check `abs(int(payload.extend_days or 0)) > 3650` is correctly placed BEFORE the no-op-payload check, so 5000 is caught even when delta=0 + meals_delta=None. Good ordering.

server.py:2293 — Error message uses Unicode minus sign U+2212 (−) not ASCII hyphen. Both render identically in JSON; tests assert on the digit '3650' so this works. If a frontend ever does substring match for '-3650' (ASCII) it would miss the lower-bound text. Minor.

Floor logic at server.py:2353 still floors via `new_end < start` — unchanged from iter-99 and verified by test_huge_negative_clamps_to_start_date with extend_days=-3000 (within cap, exceeds 30-day sub span, end_date snapped to start_date within 2s tolerance).

success_rate.frontend: not tested this iteration (review_request: backend only; UI was 9/9 in iter-99)

test_credentials: Ephemeral TEST_IT99_sess_* admin/subscriber sessions seeded in db.user_sessions inside module fixture; no human credentials required.
seed_data_creation: TEST_IT99_admin + TEST_IT99_subscriber + active subscription (sub_id TEST_IT99_sub_*, start 10d ago, end 20d future, meals_total=60). All TEST_IT99 docs purged in fixture teardown — verified via Mongo count == 0 for users/sessions/subscriptions. user_7db238e7de98 was NOT touched this iteration (only TEST_* fixture users used); its current end_date in DB is 2026-07-17 (likely state from iter-99 UI run, +/-3d from the snapshot reported there — does not affect backend tests).
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter-99 test file now has 40 tests across 8 classes: TestProfileOriginalFailingPayload, TestNameRegexAccepts (15 cross-script + Latin names), TestNameRegexRejects, TestAddressMinLength, TestPhoneValidationUnchanged, TestNegativeExtendDays, TestExtendDaysCap (4 cap-boundary tests +5000/-5000 reject, +3650/-3650 accept), TestIter98Regression. Floor test now uses extend_days=-3000 (within new cap). The cap check `abs(extend_days) > 3650` lives at server.py:2293 before the no-op check. Cross-script support comes from explicit Indic block ranges in routes/auth.py:148-164 (NOT from \p{M} since stdlib re has no Unicode property support). For any new Indic script addition, just add the block range. Run command: `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_iter99_profile_and_negative_extend.py -v` — should be 40/40 in <1s.
rca of the issue: Not applicable — no failures this iteration. Previous iter-99 RCA (Devanagari matras failing because Python \w excludes Mark categories Mn/Mc) has been resolved by main agent via explicit Indic Unicode block ranges in _NAME_RE. The fix is confirmed by all 10 cross-script smoke names returning 200, and no regression in Latin-script name acceptance (Mary-Anne O'Brien, Dr. A.P.J. Kalam, 'Rohan 23' all still pass).
